examples.title: Kruskal's Algorithm
examples.description: Greedy algorithm using Union-Find to construct MST by processing edges in ascending order
examples.code:
KruskalMST(G):
  // G = (V, E) weighted connected graph
  SORT E by weight in ascending order
  
  FOR each vertex v in V:
    MAKE-SET(v)
  
  MST = empty set
  
  FOR each edge (u, v) in sorted E:
    IF FIND-SET(u) ≠ FIND-SET(v):
      ADD edge (u, v) to MST
      UNION(u, v)
    
    IF |MST| = |V| - 1:
      BREAK
  
  RETURN MST
examples.explanation: Edges are sorted by weight. For each edge, we check if it connects two different components using Union-Find. If yes, we add it to MST and merge components. We stop when MST has n-1 edges.

examples.title: Prim's Algorithm in Python
examples.description: Implementation of Prim's algorithm using a priority queue to grow MST from a starting vertex
examples.explanation: Starting from vertex 0, we maintain a min-heap of edges from the MST to unvisited vertices. We extract the minimum, add it to MST, and update heap with edges from the newly added vertex. Uses O(E log V) time.

examples.title: Union-Find Data Structure
examples.description: Implementation of Union-Find with path compression and union by rank, used in Kruskal's algorithm
examples.explanation: Union-Find supports three operations: makeSet initializes each vertex as separate component, find locates the representative of a set (with path compression for efficiency), and union merges two sets (using union by rank for balanced trees). These operations run in O(α(n)) amortized time, where α is the inverse Ackermann function.

examples.title: Kruskal's Algorithm in Java
examples.description: Complete implementation of Kruskal's algorithm using the Edge class and Union-Find
examples.explanation: This Java implementation shows Edge class implementing Comparable for sorting, Union-Find with path compression and union by rank, and the main KruskalMST method that sorts edges and builds MST using Union-Find to detect cycles. Time: O(E log V), Space: O(V + E).
